Ensure that all necessary dependencies are installed, including keras-cv and other required libraries
Set up a virtual environment if not already done: `python -m venv stable-diffusion-env` and activate it
Install dependencies within the virtual environment using pip: `pip install tensorflow==2.14.0 keras-cv`
Clone the keras-io repository from GitHub to your local machine: `git clone https://github.com/keras-team/keras-io.git`
Create a Python script named `bug_reproducer.py` in this directory
Copy and paste the given code snippet into `bug_reproducer.py`, ensuring it is fully self-contained and imports all required modules from keras
Set specific values for any variables used within the code, such as image dimensions, batch size, learning rate, etc.
Call the main function or entry point of the example code to simulate running the fine-tuning process
Monitor the console output during execution to observe any errors or exceptions that occur
Take note of the exact error message and traceback if an exception is thrown
Ensure that all required input data, such as images or pre-trained models, are available in the correct format and location
Re-run the code multiple times if necessary to confirm the reproducibility of the bug
Verify that the issue can be consistently reproduced on different machines or environments (if applicable)
